# Implementation Plan: Basic Item Management

**Branch**: `002-item-management` | **Date**: 2025-11-24 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/002-item-management/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` command. See `.specify/templates/commands/plan.md` for the execution workflow.

## Summary

This feature provides comprehensive item management within collections: adding, viewing, editing, and removing items. It builds on Feature 001's collection infrastructure, adding flexible attribute definitions based on item types (comic books have issue numbers, vinyl has artist/album, etc.). The feature handles large collections (up to 10,000 items per collection) with pagination and enforces a configurable user limit of 50,000 total items across all collections.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: C# 14 with .NET 10 SDK
**Primary Dependencies**: .NET Aspire, Blazor Server, Entity Framework Core, ASP.NET Core Web API, MediatR (domain events)
**Storage**: Azure SQL Database (production), SQL Server LocalDB (development)
**Testing**: TUnit (unit/integration tests), bUnit (Blazor component tests), Playwright (E2E tests), FluentAssertions
**Target Platform**: Azure Container Apps (Aspire-based distributed application), web browsers (Blazor UI)
**Project Type**: Web application (Blazor frontend + ASP.NET Core Web API backend) - extending Feature 001 architecture
**Performance Goals**:
- API response time P95 <200ms for item CRUD operations
- List 1,000 items per collection page with <2 second load time
- Support collections with 10,000+ items through pagination
- Support 1,000 requests/second per service
**Constraints**:
- Maximum 50,000 total items per user (configurable)
- Pagination required for collections >100 items
- Memory baseline <512MB per service
**Scale/Scope**:
- Average 50-500 items per collection
- Edge case: 10,000 items per collection
- 50,000 total items per user limit
- Type-specific attributes (extensible schema)

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

### Test-Driven Development
✅ **PASS** - Will implement TDD Red-Green-Refactor cycle; tests written before implementation

### Code Quality & Standards
✅ **PASS** - Following .NET 10 and constitution conventions:
- Extends Feature 001 architecture (DDD, SOLID, Blazor, Tailwind CSS)
- Item as aggregate root with type-specific value objects
- Repository pattern for Item aggregate
- Continuation of existing bounded context or new bounded context ("Item Management")

### Comprehensive Testing
✅ **PASS** - Multi-layered testing:
- Unit tests for Item aggregate, ItemAttribute value objects, validation logic
- Integration tests for API endpoints, repository, pagination
- UI component tests (bUnit) for item list, item form, item detail components
- E2E tests (Playwright) for add item, edit item, view items workflows
- Performance tests for large collection pagination
- Target: 80%+ code coverage

### User Experience Consistency
✅ **PASS** - Consistent with Feature 001:
- RESTful API design with Problem Details
- WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility
- Consistent validation and error messaging
- Pagination for large datasets
- Response times <2s for collection views

### Performance Requirements
✅ **PASS** - Performance targets:
- API P95 <200ms
- Pagination for 10,000+ item collections
- Lazy loading for item details
- Database indexes on foreign keys and frequently queried fields
- BenchmarkDotNet for critical path profiling

### Simplicity & Maintainability
✅ **PASS** - Simple, focused design:
- YAGNI: Implementing only specified requirements
- Type-specific attributes using JSON column (avoiding EAV anti-pattern)
- Minimal abstraction: Repository for Item aggregate
- No premature optimization

### Security, Resilience & Observability
✅ **PASS** - Production-ready:
- **Security**: Authentication/authorization (users can only manage their own items), input validation, SQL injection prevention via EF Core
- **Observability**: Serilog structured logging, OpenTelemetry tracing, Application Insights
- **Resilience**: Polly retry policies, circuit breakers
- **Disaster Recovery**: Azure SQL backups, soft delete mechanism (Feature 003)

### Cost Optimization
✅ **PASS** - Cost-aware:
- Extends existing infrastructure from Feature 001
- Pagination reduces data transfer costs
- Application Insights sampling (5 items/sec)
- Azure SQL indexing strategy for query optimization

**Gate Status: ✅ ALL GATES PASSED - Proceeding to Phase 0 Research**

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/002-item-management/
├── plan.md              # This file
├── research.md          # Phase 0 output
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1 output
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1 output
└── contracts/           # Phase 1 output
    ├── items-api.yaml          # OpenAPI spec for Items API
    └── README.md               # Contract documentation
```

### Source Code (extends Feature 001 structure)

```text
src/
├── SuperDuperRescueHeads.Domain/            # Domain layer
│   ├── Collections/                         # Existing from Feature 001
│   └── Items/                               # NEW - Item Management context
│       ├── Item.cs                          # Aggregate root
│       ├── ItemAttribute.cs                 # Value object for type-specific attributes
│       ├── ItemName.cs                      # Value object
│       ├── IItemRepository.cs               # Repository interface
│       └── ItemDomainEvents.cs              # Domain events
├── SuperDuperRescueHeads.Infrastructure/    # Infrastructure layer
│   ├── Data/
│   │   ├── ApplicationDbContext.cs          # Extended with Items DbSet
│   │   ├── Configurations/
│   │   │   └── ItemConfiguration.cs         # NEW - EF entity configuration
│   │   └── Repositories/
│   │       └── ItemRepository.cs            # NEW - Repository implementation
│   └── Migrations/                          # NEW - Item schema migration
├── SuperDuperRescueHeads.Api/               # Web API
│   ├── Endpoints/
│   │   └── ItemsEndpoints.cs                # NEW - Minimal API endpoints
│   └── Models/
│       ├── CreateItemRequest.cs             # NEW - DTOs
│       ├── UpdateItemRequest.cs
│       └── ItemResponse.cs
└── SuperDuperRescueHeads.Web/               # Blazor UI
    ├── Components/
    │   ├── Pages/
    │   │   └── Items/                       # NEW - Item pages
    │   │       ├── Index.razor              # List items in collection
    │   │       ├── Create.razor             # Add item
    │   │       ├── Detail.razor             # View item details
    │   │       ├── Edit.razor               # Edit item
    │   │       └── Delete.razor             # Delete confirmation
    │   └── Shared/
    │       ├── ItemCard.razor               # NEW - Reusable component
    │       ├── ItemForm.razor               # NEW - Form component
    │       └── ItemAttributeField.razor     # NEW - Dynamic field component
    └── Services/
        └── ItemService.cs                   # NEW - API client

tests/
├── Unit/
│   ├── Domain/
│   │   ├── ItemTests.cs                     # NEW - Aggregate tests
│   │   ├── ItemAttributeTests.cs            # NEW - Value object tests
│   │   └── ItemNameTests.cs
│   └── Services/
│       └── ItemServiceTests.cs
├── Integration/
│   ├── Api/
│   │   └── ItemsEndpointsTests.cs           # NEW - API integration tests
│   └── Database/
│       └── ItemRepositoryTests.cs           # NEW - Repository tests
├── UI/
│   ├── Components/
│   │   ├── ItemCardTests.cs                 # NEW - bUnit component tests
│   │   ├── ItemFormTests.cs
│   │   └── ItemAttributeFieldTests.cs
│   └── Pages/
│       └── ItemsIndexTests.cs
├── E2E/
│   └── UserJourneys/
│       ├── AddItemE2ETests.cs               # NEW - Playwright E2E
│       ├── EditItemE2ETests.cs
│       └── ViewItemsE2ETests.cs
└── Performance/
    └── CriticalPaths/
        └── ItemPaginationBenchmarks.cs      # NEW - Pagination performance
```

**Structure Decision**: Extends Feature 001 web application architecture. New "Items" subdomain added to Domain layer, following same DDD patterns. Items are managed via separate API endpoints and UI pages, maintaining separation of concerns. Items bounded context is closely related to Collections context (Customer-Supplier relationship).

## Complexity Tracking

> **Fill ONLY if Constitution Check has violations that must be justified**

No constitution violations detected. All complexity is justified:
- **JSON column for type-specific attributes**: Avoids Entity-Attribute-Value (EAV) anti-pattern; provides flexibility for different item types without schema migrations; validated by constitution's flexibility requirement
- **Pagination for large collections**: Required for performance with 10,000+ items; standard practice, not premature optimization
- **Bounded context relationship**: Item Management context is closely coupled with Collection Management; using Customer-Supplier pattern from DDD

**Status: No unjustified complexity**
